content:
# John Chapter 4 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_004_page_001.jpg, JHN_004_page_002.jpg, JHN_004_page_003.jpg

1. When therefore the Lord knew that the Pharisaioi heard that Yeshua makes and baptizes more disciples than Ioannes —

2. although Yeshua himself was not baptizing, but the disciples of him —

3. he left the Ioudaia and went away again into the Galilaia.

4. And it was necessary [for] him to pass through the Samareia.

5. He comes therefore to a city of the Samareia called Sychar, near the piece of land that Iakob gave to Ioseph the son of him.

6. And [the] spring of Iakob was there. The Yeshua therefore, having been wearied from the journey, was sitting thus upon the spring. The hour was about the sixth.

7. A woman of the Samareia comes to draw water. The Yeshua says to her, "Give me to drink."

8. For the disciples of him had gone away into the city that they might buy food.

9. Then the Samaritan woman says to him, "How do you, being a Ioudaios, ask to drink from me, being a Samaritan woman?" For Ioudaioi do not associate with Samaritans.

10. The Yeshua answered and said to her, "If you knew the gift of the God and who it is [that] says to you, 'Give me to drink,' you would have asked him, and he would have given to you living water."

11. She says to him, "Lord, you have no bucket and the well is deep. From where then do you have the living water?

12. Are you greater than the father of us, Iakob, who gave us the well and himself drank from it and the sons of him and the livestock of him?"

13. The Yeshua answered and said to her, "Everyone drinking from this water will thirst again.

14. But whoever drinks from the water that I will give to him will not thirst into the age, but the water that I will give to him will become in him a spring of water springing up into eternal life."

15. The woman says to him, "Lord, give to me this water, that I may not thirst nor come here to draw."

16. He says to her, "Go, call the husband of you and come here."

17. The woman answered and said, "I do not have a husband." The Yeshua says to her, "Well you said, 'I do not have a husband.'

18. For five husbands you had, and the [one] whom now you have is not the husband of you. This you have said truly."

19. The woman says to him, "Lord, I see that you are a prophet.

20. The fathers of us on this mountain worshiped, and you say that in Hierosolyma is the place where it is necessary to worship."

21. The Yeshua says to her, "Believe me, woman, that an hour comes when neither on this mountain nor in Hierosolyma will you worship the Father.

22. You worship what you do not know; we worship what we know, for the salvation is from the Ioudaioi.

23. But an hour comes, and now is, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth, for indeed the Father seeks such [ones] worshiping him.

24. God [is] Spirit, and the [ones] worshiping him must worship in spirit and truth."

25. The woman says to him, "I know that Messias comes, the [one] called Christos. When that [one] comes, he will announce to us all things."

26. The Yeshua says to her, "I am, the [one] speaking to you."

27. And upon this the disciples of him came, and they were marveling that with a woman he was speaking. Yet no one said, "What do you seek?" or "Why do you speak with her?"

28. Then the woman left the water-jar of her and went away into the city and says to the people,

29. "Come, see a man who told me all things whatsoever I did. Is this not the Christos?"

30. They went out of the city and were coming to him.

31. In the meanwhile the disciples were asking him, saying, "Rabbi, eat."

32. But he said to them, "I have food to eat that you do not know."

33. Then the disciples said to one another, "No one brought him [anything] to eat?"

34. The Yeshua says to them, "The food of me is that I do the will of the [one] having sent me and complete the work of him.

35. Do you not say, 'Still four months and the harvest comes'? Behold, I say to you, lift up the eyes of you and see the fields, that they are white for harvest.

36. Already the [one] reaping receives wages and gathers fruit into eternal life, that the [one] sowing and the [one] reaping may rejoice together.

37. For in this the saying is true, 'One is the [one] sowing and another the [one] reaping.'

38. I sent you to reap what you have not labored; others have labored, and you have entered into the labor of them."

39. And from that city many of the Samaritans believed in him because of the word of the woman testifying, "He told me all things whatsoever I did."

40. When therefore the Samaritans came to him, they were asking him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.

41. And many more believed because of the word of him,

42. and to the woman they said, "No longer because of the speaking of you do we believe, for we ourselves have heard and we know that this is truly the Savior of the world."

43. And after the two days he went out from there into the Galilaia.

44. For Yeshua himself testified that a prophet in the own homeland of him does not have honor.

45. When therefore he came into the Galilaia, the Galilaians received him, having seen all [the things] that he did in Hierosolyma at the feast, for they also went to the feast.

46. He came therefore again to Kana of the Galilaia, where he made the water wine. And there was a certain royal [official] whose son was sick in Kapernaoum.

47. This [one], having heard that Yeshua comes from the Ioudaia into the Galilaia, went to him and was asking that he come down and heal the son of him, for he was about to die.

48. Then the Yeshua said to him, "Unless you see signs and wonders, you will not believe."

49. The royal [official] says to him, "Lord, come down before the child of me dies."

50. The Yeshua says to him, "Go, the son of you lives." The man believed the word that the Yeshua said to him and went.

51. And already [while] he was going down, the slaves of him met him, saying that the boy of him lives.

52. Then he inquired from them the hour in which he got better. They said therefore to him, "Yesterday [at the] seventh hour the fever left him."

53. Then the father knew that [it was] in that hour in which the Yeshua said to him, "The son of you lives," and he believed, himself and the whole household of him.

54. This again [was the] second sign the Yeshua did, having come from the Ioudaia into the Galilaia.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "the Lord":** Sinaiticus reads ὁ κύριος ("the Lord") rather than ὁ Ἰησοῦς ("Jesus") in some manuscripts. This is a characteristically Johannine way of referring to Jesus in narrative.

2. **v.9 — "Ioudaioi do not associate with Samaritans":** This parenthetical note, likely from the evangelist, explains the ethnic-religious tension. The Samaritans were descendants of the northern Israelite tribes who had intermarried with Assyrian settlers and worshiped on Mount Gerizim rather than in Jerusalem.

3. **v.20 — "this mountain":** Mount Gerizim, where the Samaritans had their temple (destroyed by John Hyrcanus in 128 BCE but still their sacred site). The woman raises the fundamental question dividing Jews and Samaritans: where is the proper place to worship God?

4. **v.25 — "Messias":** Μεσσίας — the second of only two uses in the NT (cf. 1:41). John translates: "the one called Christ." The Samaritans expected a messianic figure they called the Taheb ("the Restorer"), based on Deuteronomy 18:15-18.

5. **v.26 — "I am":** ἐγώ εἰμι — the first of Jesus' self-revelatory "I am" statements in John. Here it functions as both simple self-identification ("It's me") and a theophanic formula echoing God's self-revelation to Moses (Exodus 3:14). The double meaning is characteristically Johannine.

6. **v.42 — "Savior of the world":** σωτὴρ τοῦ κόσμου — a title used in Hellenistic culture for rulers and gods. Here it is applied to Jesus by Samaritans — outsiders who recognize what insiders miss.

— EMET, AI Translator
